new_value: Test verification complete - dolt server fix confirmed
Fixed in commit a1cab3b: hardened entrypoint.sh with unconditional state cleanup, two-pass dolt process kill, and final sql-server.info removal before server start

old_value: {"id":"devenv-71n","title":"Bug: allocate_port calls bare python3 which is not installed","description":"bin/devenv:167-172 — allocate_port() calls bare 'python3' but python3 is not installed in the devenv container. The function always returns 1, causing SSH port allocation to silently fail and fall through to die. Project convention is to use 'uv run python3' for anything Python-related. Recommended fix: rewrite to use 'uv run python3 -c ...' per research Open Decision #6.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T11:04:39Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-03T11:04:39Z"}

new_value: Fixed: replaced bare python3 with uv run python3 in allocate_port(); shellcheck clean; committed f4556c8

old_value: {"id":"devenv-5nl","title":"Install bats-core in devenv environment","description":"bats-core is not installed. Add it to the devenv Dockerfile (via shared/tools/ or docker/devenv/) so it is always available in containers. This is the prerequisite for all test execution. See research Open Decision #1 for installation method options.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T11:03:12Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-03T11:03:12Z"}

Fixed: LOG_LEVEL corrected to DEVENV_LOG_LEVEL on line 138; log_primitives.bats added to test layout section

old_value: {"id":"devenv-mw1","title":"Create test helper: tests/helpers/test_env.bash","description":"Create setup/teardown helper per spec determinism rules. setup: create temp HOME (mktemp -d), set HOME, LC_ALL=C, PATH with tests/fixtures/bin first, export DEVENV_HOME to repo root. teardown: remove temp HOME. This is the first test file to create and is required by all .bats files.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T11:03:20Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-03T11:03:20Z"}

Required by CLI contract test files.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T11:03:22Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-03T11:03:22Z"}

{"id":"devenv-0mm","title":"Create fake docker fixture: tests/fixtures/bin/docker","description":"Executable bash script that logs arguments to DOCKER_LOG or BATS_TMPDIR/docker_calls.log. Returns exit 0 by default. Supports FAKE_DOCKER_EXIT (override exit code) and FAKE_DOCKER_OUTPUT (canned stdout) env vars. Must handle subcommands: ps, stop, run, exec, build, info, images, volume, system. Start minimal, extend as tests require. Required by all CLI contract tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T11:03:26Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-03T11:03:26Z"}

Created tests/helpers/assert_output.bash with all required helpers

old_value: {"id":"devenv-kmi","title":"Extract duplicated primitives to shared library","description":"resolve_project_path and derive_project_image_suffix are duplicated character-for-character in both bin/devenv and bin/build-devenv. Per spec section 4: test each copy independently 'until the duplication is resolved by extracting a shared library.' This task extracts both functions to a shared bash library (e.g., shared/bash/primitives.sh) and updates both scripts to source it.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T11:04:43Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-03T11:04:43Z"}

new_value: Extracted resolve_project_path and derive_project_image_suffix to shared/bash/primitives.sh; both bin/devenv and bin/build-devenv now source it; unit tests added in tests/bats/shared_primitives.bats; all 72 bats tests pass, shellcheck clean.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-72c","title":"No Makefile or CI pipeline for running tests","description":"The testing spec requires tests to be fast and runnable without Docker, but there is no Makefile, no 'make test' target, and no .github/workflows/ CI pipeline. Tests must be invoked manually with 'bats tests/bats/'. This means tests are never run automatically on commits/PRs, which defeats the purpose of having them. A Makefile with at minimum a 'test' target and ideally a CI workflow should be added.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:08Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:08Z"}

new_value: Added Makefile with test and lint targets; added .github/workflows/ci.yml running both on push and PR

old_value: {"id":"devenv-ki1","title":"Duplicate validate_docker() definition in bin/build-devenv","description":"bin/build-devenv defines validate_docker() twice (lines 58–67 and 70–79). The second definition silently overwrites the first. The comment above the second definition ('Build the repo-base image') belongs to build_stage_repo_base(), indicating a copy-paste mistake. Both definitions are identical, so behavior is currently unaffected, but this is a code quality bug detectable by shellcheck (SC2329 is suppressed, but the duplicate definition itself is an issue).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:04Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:04Z"}

new_value: Removed duplicate validate_docker() definition (lines 70-79). Single definition remains at lines 58-67. shellcheck passes.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-mqq","title":"devenv volume rm --all --force test has a weak assertion (accepts exit 0 or 1)","description":"tests/bats/devenv_cli.bats test 'devenv volume rm --all --force: exits 0 and fake docker called volume rm' has a comment admitting it 'may not fully exercise the rm path' and asserts '[[ status -eq 0 || status -eq 1 ]]' — i.e., any exit code is acceptable. This is not a real test. The fix requires improving the fake docker fixture to return different output per subcommand so that 'docker volume ls' returns a volume name while subsequent calls (ps, inspect, rm) return appropriate responses.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:27Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:27Z"}

new_value: Fixed: enhanced fake docker fixture with per-subcommand FAKE_DOCKER_<SUBCOMMAND>_OUTPUT/_EXIT env vars; rewrote the weak test to assert exit 0 and verify 'volume rm devenv-test-vol' in docker log. All 82 tests pass.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-e4l","title":"No CLI contract tests for devenv start / attach / --port flag","description":"The testing spec lists CLI contract tests as required for bin/devenv, but the start path (cmd_start) and attach path (attach_container) have zero CLI contract tests in devenv_cli.bats. The --port flag is also untested. cmd_start is the default command (invoked when a path is given) and is the most user-facing code path. At minimum: 'devenv .' should be tested with fake docker to verify docker run is called with expected flags; '--port 2222 .' should verify the SSH port flag is passed; and a re-attach to an already-running container should be tested.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:19Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:19Z"}

new_value: Added 3 CLI contract tests: devenv <path> verifies docker run, --port 2222 verifies port flag, re-attach verifies docker exec without docker run. Also extended fake docker fixture with IMAGES/RUN/EXEC per-subcommand overrides.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-bt3","title":"allocate_port test silently passes when uv is unavailable (exit-1 path untested)","description":"tests/bats/devenv_primitives.bats: 'allocate_port: returns a numeric port between 1 and 65535' silently accepts exit code 1 with the comment 'If uv is not available, exit code 1 is acceptable'. This means the test passes vacuously when uv is absent, and the error-path behavior of allocate_port (returning 1 with no output) is never explicitly asserted. Two separate tests should exist: one asserting a valid numeric port when uv IS available (uv is present in the devenv container), and one asserting exit code 1 and empty stdout when uv is absent (using a PATH that excludes uv).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T09:07:12Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T09:07:12Z"}

new_value: Split into two explicit tests: one asserting valid numeric port when uv is present, one asserting exit code 1 and empty stdout when uv is absent via restricted PATH.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-09r","title":"No tests for DEVENV_MEMORY / DEVENV_MEMORY_SWAP / DEVENV_CPUS resource override env vars","description":"bin/devenv documents DEVENV_MEMORY, DEVENV_MEMORY_SWAP, and DEVENV_CPUS as user-facing override environment variables (shown in 'devenv help' output). These are used in cmd_start/start_container when constructing docker run arguments. There are no CLI contract tests verifying that setting these env vars passes the correct flags to docker (e.g., '--memory 4g' when DEVENV_MEMORY=4g). Per the testing spec, CLI contract tests should encode docker intent. Tests using the fake docker log should be added to devenv_cli.bats.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T09:07:16Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T09:07:16Z"}

new_value: Added 4 CLI contract tests to devenv_cli.bats covering DEVENV_MEMORY, DEVENV_MEMORY_SWAP, DEVENV_CPUS overrides and default values. All 90 tests pass.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-dt8","title":"Weak fake docker fixture cannot vary behavior per subcommand","description":"tests/fixtures/bin/docker is a single fake that returns FAKE_DOCKER_OUTPUT for ALL docker calls, regardless of subcommand. This makes it impossible to write tests where different docker subcommands (e.g., 'docker volume ls', 'docker ps', 'docker volume inspect') need to return different output. The existing workaround (inline custom docker script via mktemp -d) is used in only one test (volume rm --all with in-use volume) but is duplicated effort. The fake docker should support per-subcommand output configuration, e.g., via environment variables like FAKE_DOCKER_VOLUME_LS_OUTPUT, FAKE_DOCKER_PS_OUTPUT.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:23Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:23Z"}

new_value: Fake docker fixture already had per-subcommand output/exit support via FAKE_DOCKER_<SUBCMD>_OUTPUT and FAKE_DOCKER_<SUBCMD>_EXIT env vars. Removed the only remaining mktemp workaround in devenv_cli.bats ('volume rm --all with in-use volume' test) and replaced it with FAKE_DOCKER_VOLUME_LS_OUTPUT + FAKE_DOCKER_PS_OUTPUT env vars. All 90 tests pass.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-4je","title":"Spec §4 duplication note is stale: derive_project_image_suffix already extracted to shared library","description":"specs/testing-standard.md §4 ('Primitives as Stable API') gives derive_project_image_suffix as an example of a function duplicated across scripts that 'must be tested independently until the duplication is resolved by extracting a shared library'. The duplication HAS been resolved — derive_project_image_suffix now lives in shared/bash/primitives.sh and both bin/devenv and bin/build-devenv source it. The spec text is now stale and misleading. It should be updated to remove the stale example and instead document that the shared library IS the canonical location, with both scripts sourcing it.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T09:07:08Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T09:07:08Z"}

new_value: Updated §4 of specs/testing-standard.md to remove stale duplication note and document shared/bash/primitives.sh as the canonical location, with both scripts sourcing it and tests in shared_primitives.bats.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-54g","title":"shared_primitives.bats not listed in spec test layout","description":"The testing spec (specs/testing-standard.md, Test Layout section) lists the expected bats files but does NOT include shared_primitives.bats. The implementation has this file (tests/bats/shared_primitives.bats) testing shared/bash/primitives.sh, but the spec is out of date. The spec should be updated to include shared_primitives.bats in the layout, and note shared/bash/primitives.sh under 'What To Test'.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:11Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:11Z"}

new_value: Updated specs/testing-standard.md: added shared_primitives.bats to Test Layout section and added shared/bash/primitives.sh entry under What To Test.

old_value: {"id":"devenv-2vu","title":"Testing spec references wrong Testing Trophy blog post URL","description":"specs/testing-standard.md line 152 links the 'Testing Trophy' reference to 'https://kentcdodds.com/blog/common-mistakes-with-react-testing-library' — this is the 'Common Mistakes with React Testing Library' article, NOT the Testing Trophy article. The correct URL for the Testing Trophy is https://kentcdodds.com/blog/the-testing-trophy-and-testing-classifications","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:40Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:40Z"}

new_value: Fixed: updated Testing Trophy URL in specs/testing-standard.md line 162 to https://kentcdodds.com/blog/the-testing-trophy-and-testing-classifications

old_value: {"id":"devenv-l3a","title":"devenv_cli.bats uses raw bash pattern match instead of assert_stderr_contains for error messages","description":"Several tests in devenv_cli.bats check error output using raw bash: '[[ \"${output}\" =~ \"...\"]]. This bypasses the assert helpers defined in tests/helpers/assert_output.bash and produces less readable failure messages. Per the testing standard's assertion about reducing boilerplate with helpers, these should use assert_stderr_contains or assert_stdout_contains. Affected tests: 'devenv stop (no target)', 'devenv volume (no subcommand)', 'devenv volume rm (no args)', 'devenv volume rm --all with in-use volume'.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:37Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:37Z"}

new_value: Replaced 4 raw [[ ${output} =~ ... ]] pattern matches with assert_stdout_contains in devenv_cli.bats. All 90 tests pass.

This creates a discoverability gap for developers new to the repo.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jg@gundersenj.com","created_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:38Z","created_by":"Jørgen Gundersen","updated_at":"2026-03-04T08:59:38Z"}
